gameplay / campaign
Jules Lavelle 8/11/2020 11:20 AM
Regimental Organization: https://1drv.ms/u/s!Ak4ZWeFuG2EfiL4gtHYyPWoUrJS6lA?e=30s6Vg
Jules Lavelle pinned a message to this channel. 8/11/2020 11:20 AM
Jules Lavelle 8/11/2020 1:45 PM
Summer is the regular campaign season, when the Minister of War will call up a certain number of brigades and send them to the front. Characters may also volunteer to campaign in the other seasons (Fall, Winter, Spring), along with their whole command if they hold one. NPC commanders have a chance of volunteering their commands each season on a roll of 6 on a 1d6. This roll can be modified by influence. Characters without a command may take a temporary leave-of-absence to serve in a Frontier Regiment. Characters retain their rank, or receive the rank of Private if they were not enlisted, and are paid at Gascon Regiment rates. Characters may not volunteer to campaign for less than a full month. Privates with an Endurance of 110 or more and Expertise of 9 or more are eligible to volunteer with the elite Royal Northern Highlands Brigade. Officers may also volunteer if they meet the requirements for Privates and have received one merit promotion or been mentioned in despatches. Officers with a MA of 1 are not accepted in the RNHB.

The colonel's orders are delivered by a muddy, shoeless child. Pierre reviews them, and recognizes the defensive plan for the sheer idiocy it is. Narrator 8/31/2020 10:31 PM
The frontier regiments have suffered a crushing defeat at the hands of the rebel leaders Donat and Barran. Their names are growing in popularity, and the rebellion threatens to spread as nearby peasantry wonder if they too can escape the ruinous tax burdens of the King.
10:32 PM
The King charges the Minister of War to raise a great army to quell the rebellion once and for all.

Narrator 9/6/2020 11:33 PM
@Jean Renault , Mlle. Caillet has been writing you every day. Her letters rejuvenate you and bring you the courage needed to face battle. She misses a day in early July, and you spend the rest of the week fretting. But then her letters resume, more full of love and heartache than ever.

Narrator 9/9/2020 10:30 PM
If you choose poltroonery, you may add any number you feel necessary to the die roll needed for death. You then roll a d6 -- on a 1 or 2, your cowardice escapes notice, but you miss the chance for plunder (promotion/despatches are rolled as normal). On a 3, 4, or 5, you are seen, and lose twice the modification you took in SP every month going forward until you are mentioned in despatches. On a 6, you are immediately dismissed from the regiment and may not apply to any regiment until you've been mentioned in despatches while in a Frontier Regiment. Your old regiment will never accept you again. You also suffer the above SP penalty.
10:30 PM
If you choose reckless bravery, you may subtract any number you wish from the die roll needed for death. You will use that modifier on the die rolls for despatches, promotion, and plunder as well.
10:31 PM
you will, unmodified, roll for Mention against a 12, Promotion against a 7, and Plunder against an 11.

I choose reckless bravery modifier of 4.

As the Cavalry Division advances, the Guards Brigade moves in from the other side. Narrator 9/9/2020 11:35 PM
The 3rd and 4th Brigade of Foot rally together. The Royal Northern Highlands Brigade and Frontier Regiments swing in from the side. The valiant sacrifice of the 1st Division is sufficient to enable the 2nd Division and 3rd Army to perform a classic pincer maneuver. The rebel army is wiped from the surface of the Earth...but at what cost?

Curtis Sinnoch 9/26/2020 9:41 PM
awwww yisssssss
Narrator 9/26/2020 9:43 PM
The Frontier Regiment has been assigned to a Siege mission. They will attempt to capture the forts of Valtellina.
9:43 PM
Blastagarded 9/26/2020 9:44 PM
Narrator 9/26/2020 9:46 PM
@Curtis Sinnoch , @Ghôst Touchard , and @Anonymouse are fighting. Curtis serves under his regiment. Guy and Alphonse have been placed into the chain of command of the Frontier Regiment itself.
9:50 PM
The two regiments operate independently, with little communication. Col. Denis Nee, commanding the Queen's Own Carabiniers, is a leader of great foresight and reliability. He convinces the Frontier Regiment's commander, Col. Rémy Aymard, to conduct a frontal attack to try to break the siege, while the Q.O.C. scale the walls unnoticed.
9:52 PM
The defenses have been battered by weeks of blockade and cannonade, but the walls remain intact. The Frontier Regiment rushes toward the gates under heavy fire. "My life for France!" screams Col. Aymard.
9:53 PM
The frazzled, exhausted Spanish defenders may not understand the shout, but they know too well its meaning.
9:53 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 9/26/2020 9:53 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (3) = 3
Narrator 9/26/2020 9:54 PM
Col. Aymard's orders are solid, but no less solid than the gates themselves. The attackers are repelled, but in good order, and return to their lines mostly intact. The outcome of the Frontier Regiment's role in the siege is Inconclusive.
9:57 PM
@Ghôst Touchard, roll for Death against an 11, Reckless Bravery or Poltroonery notwithstanding.

Narrator 9/26/2020 10:15 PM
As the fighting against the gate reaches its peak, around the far side of the fort, Col. Nee gives the order for his men to deploy grapnels and begin their ascent.
10:15 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 9/26/2020 10:15 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (5) = 5
Narrator 9/26/2020 10:17 PM
The men throw with all their might, but there are few defenses at this part of the wall, and therefore little purchase. A few defenders throw rocks, but there aren't enough targets on the wall for it to be very dangerous. After thirty minutes of fruitless effort, Col. Nee calls off the attack.
10:18 PM
The Queen's Own Carabiniers' role in the siege is Inconclusive.
10:19 PM

Ghôst Touchard 10/12/2020 11:49 PM

Jules Lavelle 10/12/2020 11:49 PM

11:50 PM
2
Ghôst Touchard 10/12/2020 11:50 PM
Blastagarded 10/13/2020 12:07 AM
Hercule sees the stream of the young men of France heading out to campaign. The question of @Jules Lavelle regarding his service to France echoing in his ear. The fame that such service could garner echoing even louder "I have my commission now," he mutters to himself, "and one can only get so far on carousing and politicking... no sense leaving all the glory to these lot." He writes two letters. The first, instructions to his staff regarding upkeep of the estate while he is away. The second, a brief note to Alida explaining his absence and that he will keep the calm understanding of her company within his heart. A moment of regretful emotion crosses his face before returning to its normal steel-eyed gaze. He dons his uniform, gives himself a steady once over in the full length mirror, and heads out to war. (edited)
2
1

Narrator 10/17/2020 9:56 PM
After a decisive naval engagement between the Royal Fleet commanded by the Duke of Nevers and the Huguenot Navy under the Duke of Soubise, the rebels found their backs broken by the brilliant maneuvers of Nevers, who said "Never!" to the chances of Huguenot independence.
4
9:57 PM
The Huguenots retreat into the fort in Blavet harbor. The Duke of Vendôme is assigned to conduct an amphibious landing and take the fort.
10:01 PM
Vendôme commands the 53rd and his own battalions to storm the fort directly. The Royal Foot Guards will wait in reserve and move to take advantage of any sudden gaps in the defenses.

Narrator 10/18/2020 12:15 AM
Hercule's battalion, without horses, is utterly unprepared for the intensity of fighting amphibiously. They suffer horrible losses as they find themselves repelled back into the sea.
12:16 AM
Hercule, your rolls are Death: 11, Mention: 10, Promotion: 9. No chance for loot.
Blastagarded 10/18/2020 12:58 AM
Hercule's mind races with adrenaline, the likes of which he has never experience before. He knew his orders were folly, but he lost the nerve to question them. His normally sharp mind distracted by the unrelenting input of the pure chaos of battle. With failure and death clear within his sight, he steadied himself with a breath, clenched his sword, and did what he could to organize a fighting retreat (OC: Reckless Bravery 1)
12:59 AM
/roll 2d6 #death

Blastagarded 10/18/2020 1:04 AM
catching his breath when the battle was over It appears war is just like love. The newness of the first time carries with it so much... it can overwhelm the sense. But now I know.
2
1
Narrator 10/18/2020 1:07 AM
The fighting outside the fortress is madness. Men pour from boats and the fort, scream, and die. Great struggles are waged over bare feet of land between the sea and fortress walls. Although the armies of France are valiant, they are unable to make real progress in seizing the fortress. They hold position throughout the day, but as night falls, each battalion slips back to its boats and retreats from the harbor. The Huguenots are victorious. Duke Soubise names himself "Admiral of the Protestant Church."

Narrator 11/8/2020 3:12 PM
@Curtis Sinnoch and @Blastagarded have volunteered on campaign.
3:14 PM
Each leads his battalion under the Frontier Regiment Colonel, who is Colonel Christian Solé this season.
3:14 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/8/2020 3:14 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (5) = 5
Narrator 11/8/2020 3:14 PM
The Spring campaign is a Defense mission.
3:16 PM
The Huguenots, fresh from their victory at Blavet, have raised a large army. Their navy has already set sail, and the army now travels south, seeking to link up with the larger part of the Huguenot force at La Rochelle.
3:17 PM
The Frontier Regiments have been deployed to Nantes to set up a defensive line. Success here will slow or halt the Huguenot advance. But the Protestants outnumber the Catholics three to one. Their approaching flags seem to stretch forever.
3:18 PM
Colonel Solé, clear-eyed and hale, delivers a speech in which he declares that the enemy may be numerous, but quality is on the side of the French Army.
3:18 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/8/2020 3:18 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (2) = 2
Narrator 11/8/2020 3:20 PM
Col. Solé battle result: 5
3:21 PM
Unfortunately, the valorous part of the French Army is back in Paris. Mid-speech, Solé notices the Huguenots have begun their charge. He spurs his horse...away from the battlefield, leaving his adjutant to give orders.
3:21 PM
@Curtis Sinnoch and @Blastagarded , roll to determine your battle results as your positions are overrun by hordes of screaming Protestants.

Jules Lavelle 11/8/2020 9:13 PM
Blastagarded 11/8/2020 9:14 PM
The knighthood was an unexpected boost, but it has been quite the slog going from SL3 to SL10.
Curtis Sinnoch 11/8/2020 9:15 PM

Jules Lavelle 11/8/2020 9:15 PM
no time tonight but I might be able to get started with it tomorrow
Blastagarded 11/8/2020 9:16 PM

Ghôst Touchard 11/8/2020 9:16 PM

Narrator 11/9/2020 12:30 AM
Yes, we are going to do the whole summer this week.
@Narrator
Narrator 11/9/2020 12:26 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/9/2020 12:26 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (1) = 1
Jean Renault 11/9/2020 12:26 PM
Whos this for?
Narrator 11/9/2020 12:27 PM
Despite his poor showing last summer, the Field Marshal has placed the Field Army under the command of General Gilles Barbier once more. (edited)
4
Curtis Sinnoch 11/9/2020 12:27 PM
@Jean Renault this is for Fame, Fortune, and the glory of Paris!
laiv.wire 11/9/2020 12:29 PM
It's for your maman.
2
Narrator 11/9/2020 8:40 PM
The Summer Campaign begins.
4
2
8:43 PM
The Huguenot forces have spread throughout Western France, controlling the coastline north of Aquitaine. Even Bordeaux has fallen after the disastrous Spring Campaign.
8:44 PM
With the French Navy mostly wiped out in the Winter Campaign, Cardinal Richelieu has made serious concessions to England and the Dutch in return for assistance against their Protestant brothers, in the form of loaned ships to be manned by French crews.
8:45 PM
As Vice-Admiral Van Dorp roams up and down the Atlantic coast, seeking to engage the Protestant Navy, the Field Army of France has been deployed to root out and eliminate the scattered Protestant militias which have taken hold of the coast.
8:47 PM
Unable to maintain significant tactical control during such a far-ranging campaign, General Barbier is relegated to making high-level operational and strategic decisions. His adjutant, the colonel of the 69th Arquebusiers, is well-known for being a very large fish from a very small pond.
8:48 PM
The General gathers his division commanders around him and gives them their orders.
2
8:48 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/9/2020 8:48 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (4) = 4
Narrator 11/9/2020 8:49 PM
While uninspired, the General's command that divisions are to move as one, so that they can reinforce each other should one falter, is what the textbook says, at least where it comes to open-field warfare. The area being heavily forested is no deterrent.
2
8:49 PM
The result is Inconclusive.
8:52 PM
1st Division Commander Lt. General Pierre-Paul Auclair has been assigned the honor of leading the center. A dependable but unimaginable soldier, he passes the General Barbier's orders on to his brigadiers and say "You know what to do."
8:52 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/9/2020 8:52 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (3) = 3
Narrator 11/9/2020 8:53 PM
Advancing forward steadily, the occasional encampment is found and destroyed, but many more are found abandoned, the militias long having made their escape.
8:53 PM
The result is Inconclusive.
8:55 PM
2nd Division Commander Lt. General Hervé Dufau is General Barbier's nephew, and no better suited to his position than his uncle. He orders his men to set fire to the woods behind the division, the better to spur them forward.
2
1
8:55 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/9/2020 8:55 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (4) = 4
Narrator 11/9/2020 8:56 PM
His adjutant reassures the brigadiers that Dufau was merely kidding. The men continue the steady advance.
8:56 PM
The result is Inconclusive.
8:57 PM
The Frontier Division, with its new recruit @Careless Ocelot, is split up and scattered in front of the other divisions, to act as forward scouts.
8:58 PM
Its commander this season, Jean-François Vayssière, is a sentenced prisoner of some high status, but a military man by no means.
8:58 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/9/2020 8:58 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (2) = 2
Narrator 11/9/2020 8:59 PM
When asked by the Frontier Regiment's Colonel how to best advance, he is heard to retort, "Certainly not by joining the Frontier Regiment, sir."
1
1
8:59 PM
The Frontier Division is picked apart and driven from the field by camouflaged rebels.
9:01 PM
The Cavalry Division has been assigned the left flank. Lt. General Jérémie De Michele instructs his men to dismount and lead their horses until the battle is joined, to keep pace with the other flanks as ordered.
9:01 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/9/2020 9:01 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (6) = 6
Narrator 11/9/2020 9:04 PM
The enemy are quick to note the approaching horses, but not as quick as if the horses were being ridden hard. It is all too easy to run down the fleeing rebels as they hurriedly pack their camps. The enemy on the left flank is driven from the field.
9:04 PM
The Dragoon Brigade, reporting directly to the General, is tasked with responding quickly to fill any holes, and so deployed to cover for the retreating Frontier Division.
9:05 PM
Bg. General Sylvestre Laurens isn't excited about these orders, but not much excites the old salt anymore.
9:05 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/9/2020 9:05 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (1) = 1
Narrator 11/9/2020 9:07 PM
Unlike most of the cavalry brigades, the dragoons are trained to dismount before engaging. They find themselves shot to pieces as they struggle to leap off their horses safely in the heavy forest. They, too, are driven from the field.
9:09 PM
The Guards Brigade have seen little trouble. Bg. General Yvan Bellefeuille is dissatisfied with this, and hungers for glory. He orders the Guards to fire indiscriminately into the woods to frighten the rebels into surrender.
9:09 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/9/2020 9:09 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (1) = 1
Narrator 11/9/2020 9:11 PM
The Guards pick their targets with great relish. The targets turn out to be retreating members of the Frontier Division, who scream for help as they die. This in turn draws out the Dragoon Brigade, who enter into heavy combat with the Guards. The Dragoons enjoy a pyrrhic victory. The Guards are crushed.
2
9:13 PM
The 1st Brigade of Foot, led by the redoubtable Bg. General Robert Vasseur, sees the ruin facing the center flank. Vasseur realizes the success of the day rides on his shoulders.
9:13 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/9/2020 9:13 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (4) = 4
Narrator 11/9/2020 9:14 PM
Some are lost, but many are saved. Vasseur leads his men to a fighting stalemate, and at the very least, holds the ground.
9:14 PM
The result is Inconclusive.
9:16 PM
On the right flank, the 2nd Brigade commander, Bg. Gen. Xavier Cherbuliez, receives word of a fortified position just ahead, atop a large hill. There is mention of cannons.
1
9:16 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/9/2020 9:16 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (3) = 3
Narrator 11/9/2020 9:17 PM
The mention turns out to be correct. Cherbuliez tries to array his regiments for an assault on the hill, but organization in the forest is too much of a challenge even for his adroit military mind. Some cannons pick off a few men, but the rest take cover, unable to advance. The result is Inconclusive.
9:18 PM
The 3rd Brigade of Foot, under the command of the substantially-less-able Bg. Gen Auguste Delacroix, sees the same hill from another angle.
9:18 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/9/2020 9:18 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (2) = 2
Narrator 11/9/2020 9:19 PM
Delacroix trusts in his men to organize themselves. But little is more organized than a cannonball careening through ranks of hapless footmen. The 3rd Brigade suffers heavy casualties, and is driven from the field.
2
9:20 PM
As his men run screaming into death at the hands of the celebrated Guards Brigade, Col. Yvan Grignard of the Frontier Regiment knows he is not meant to go out this way. There are simply too many ladies left to woo.
2
1
9:20 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/9/2020 9:20 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (2) = 2
Narrator 11/9/2020 9:21 PM
He orders his men to play dead, and some listen, perhaps even @Careless Ocelot. The result is Inconclusive.
9:23 PM
On the left flank, the leader of the Horse Guards, Bg. Gen. Honoré Boulle, drives his men ever forward in pursuit of the escaping rebels. Mid-gallop, his keen eyes note something shining to the north.
9:23 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/9/2020 9:23 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (1) = 1
Narrator 11/9/2020 9:24 PM
A previously unnoticed cannon emplacement blows his horse out from under him. Suddenly the cavalry charge becomes a rout, as the screams of dying equines and men together fill the air.
2
2
9:26 PM
The Heavy Brigade's acting commander, Col. Gaël Roquebert, has no idea what he's doing. Thankfully, his adjutant is a man of masterful experience.
9:26 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/9/2020 9:26 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (5) = 5
Narrator 11/9/2020 9:27 PM
Roquebert and his adjutant steer their charge around the exploding Horse Guards, conducting a pincer maneuver that wipes out the dangerous cannon emplacement, conclusively demonstrating the power of well-led cavalry.
9:29 PM
Amidst the gruesome deaths of his fellow Guards, Colonel Fernand Brunet of the King's Musketeers resolves that honor demands no lesser regiment best his battalions.
9:30 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/9/2020 9:30 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (5) = 5
Narrator 11/9/2020 9:30 PM
He lines his men up and sends shot after shot into the maddened Dragoons. It is enough to slow their advance. The result is Inconclusive.
1
9:33 PM
Down the line, the Crown Prince Cuirassiers acting commander, Lt. Col. Morin, orders them to ride down every last fleeing militiaman. "No quarter!" he screams, even over the thunder of hooves and cannon.
9:33 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/9/2020 9:33 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (3) = 3
Narrator 11/9/2020 9:34 PM
Many are slain, but the bulk of the enemy are able to use the cover of the forest to escape. The result is Inconclusive.
9:34 PM
@Blastagarded , roll to determine your Battle Result.
Blastagarded 11/9/2020 9:34 PM
/roll 1d6 #MA7
Dice Golem BOT 11/9/2020 9:34 PM
​Hercule Bonaparte rolled 1d6: (1) = 1
1
Narrator 11/9/2020 9:36 PM
The 3rd Squadron rides directly into a trap. Tripwires strung low across the forest paths send many of your companies to the ground, where the Huguenots set upon them with spears.
2
2
9:37 PM
Hercule's intuition, however, warned him to divide his troops widely, and the militiamen are unable to reach the majority before they can regroup and ride away. The result is Inconclusive.

It appears incompetence is infectious in battle. I will have to make adjustments to compensate, should I see tomorrow.

Narrator 11/10/2020 1:53 PM
The Summer Campaign is a disaster. Thousands of men are slaughtered in the woods outside Nantes, picked apart by an underequipped Huguenot force that nevertheless knows the locale all too well. With the vast Protestant army at its gates, La Rochelle's city council votes to join Duke Soubise. August 8th marks the formation of the French Protestant Republic, with Soubise anointed as Lord Protector.
3
Jean Renault 11/15/2020 8:47 PM
receives a letter from Mlle Blaise informing him that she could no longer stand waiting, after four long months of being unable to see him due to military service. She mentioned in passing being enamored with a new suitor, a Mr. Armand Baschet. It was a name that seemed to ring a distant bell, but now was one committed to memory. (edited)
Ghôst Touchard 11/15/2020 9:24 PM
@Ghôst Touchard receives a letter from the bawdyhouse informing him that after four long months, his spot on the wall of 'customer of the year' has been replaced by another John. Also they seem to have fewer crabs (perhaps due to his absence.)
2
Anonymouse 11/16/2020 11:07 AM
@Jean Renault, you have my sincerest apologies that your great service to France is causing such sacrifice on your end. If I may help in any way, please let me know.
Jean Renault 11/16/2020 11:16 AM
@Anonymouse C'est la vie, as I will continue in my duty to France! Though, I would ask that we may return to Paris, even for one Season, following this campaign, so that I may... resolve some outstanding affairs should we survive (edited)
Anonymouse 11/16/2020 1:13 PM
But of course! It would be terrible to be out on campaign during the holidays.
Ghôst Touchard 11/16/2020 1:29 PM
The holidays.. Yes. Best not to be out on campaign, especially with the anniversary of your father's passing. If we survive this sortie, allow me to buy you a round, @Anonymouse. From what I heard, your father was the epitome of a good man.
Anonymouse 11/21/2020 9:12 AM
Thank you, @Ghôst Touchard. He truly was.
Blastagarded 11/27/2020 11:14 AM
@Curtis Sinnoch @Jules Lavelle and the rest of the Royal Foot Guard: I trust you all took advantage of your respite in October. Now let us go and remind France and the world that it has one of the greatest fighting forces on the planet.

Jules Lavelle 11/27/2020 12:06 PM

Narrator 12/2/2020 10:35 AM
The Protestant Navy, having taken the coast, quickly moved to establish control over strategic islands it could use as ports of refuge from royal reinforcements. One island in particular, Île de Ré, has become a central gathering point from which the Huguenots launch raids on unsuspecting French towns.
10:36 AM
The Dutch and English have once again lent their warships to the cause. the Duke of Montmorency requests the Frontier Regiments to aid in capturing Ré.
10:36 AM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 12/2/2020 10:36 AM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (1) = 1
1
Narrator 12/2/2020 10:40 AM
A fierce naval battle ensues. Although several Dutch ships are lost with all hands, the Protestant Navy is finally crushed. Soubise retreats to the fortress on the island's northwest point. English ships deliver the Frontier Regiments to the island's unprotected southern beaches and wish them Godspeed.
10:40 AM
The Frontier Regiments will undertake a Siege. (edited)
Narrator 12/2/2020 10:50 AM
Col. Jean-Pascal Boucher, currently assigned to lead the Frontier Regiment, is a man of uncommon tactical brilliance who happened to tell an off-color joke about the Queen at the wrong time.
2
1
10:52 AM
Cols. Boucher and Devereux, alongside Lt. Col. Bonaparte, agree to arrange their forces into a trident, with Boucher leading the central thrust and the Royal Regiments on the flanks.
10:55 AM
The island offers poor cover for advancing on a fortified position. Boucher orders his battalions into loose formations, to diminish the effectiveness of enemy batteries.
10:56 AM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 12/2/2020 10:56 AM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (6) = 6
Narrator 12/2/2020 10:58 AM
The tactic works even better than expected. The Frontier Regiment attains the walls of the fort without suffering a single casualty, as the panicking defenders have failed to properly calibrate their cannons.
10:58 AM
The Frontier Regiments have succeeded in their portion of the Siege, with a result of 1.

Blastagarded 12/2/2020 11:18 AM
Alright men, the enemy is cornered, so now is the time for patience. There is no need for unnecessary recklessness, we control the tempo, not them. And while I know many of you will wish this as an opportunity for vengeance, when you encounter cowards you are to show them mercy and take them prisoner. Do not give our quarry reason to believe they must fight in desperation to the last. Show them a golden bridge on which they may have some hope of seeing tomorrow and their morale shall crumble with barely any effort on our part. (edited)

/roll 1d6

In the middle of the victory party, the 2nd Battalion Commander of the Cardinal's Guard and his captains sneak away from the official celebration to break into the Duke of Soubise's wine supply. The Winter Campaign
8:49 PM
Aided by the Dutch and English ships, in the wake of the capture of the Duke of Soubise, the last remaining Protestant strongholds have been captured and demilitarized. All that remains is to scour the countryside for scattered pockets of traitor militia.
8:50 PM
/roll 1d6

​Narrator rolled 1d6: (6) = 6
Narrator 12/30/2020 8:50 PM
As such, all regiments will be performing Field Operations, hunting down the traitors wherever they may be found along the West Coast.
8:54 PM
@Blastagarded , @Curtis Sinnoch , and @Jules Lavelle are deployed with the rest of the Royal Foot Guards. @Anonymouse and @harry are attached to the Frontier Regiment under the command of Col. Jean-Yves De la Croix, currently serving out a sentence for mass public indecency for a drunken Noel dare gone wrong.
3
4
3
8:58 PM
Independently ranging, the Royal Foot Guards happen upon a contingent of Protestant troops waiting to ferry across the Sèvre Niortaise. Once across, the traitors are sure to burn the boats, eliminating any chance of capture. @Blastagarded, what happens next is up to you.
Blastagarded 12/30/2020 9:18 PM
@Jules Lavelle Prepare and send dispatches for a full assault on their flank opposite the Frontier Regiment. Tell the men to offer no quarter and an make examples of those who do not flee in terror. We shall drive them from the shoreline away from their boats in the waiting arms of capture by the Frontier Regiment. And, if any do elude us, the memory of what happens to traitors will be forever etched into their minds, such that they will never raise a hand against France again. Task and position @Curtis Sinnoch with the important duty of securing any path back to their boats once we have pushed them away from the shore. Last thing we want is some thinking they can double back.
Jules Lavelle 12/30/2020 9:25 PM
Yes, sir! On the nonce!

Narrator 12/30/2020 9:34 PM
The enemy stands utterly unprepared to do battle. The first volley launched from ambush rips through the men already on boats, leaving their broken bodies strewn across the damaged rivercraft. The rest of the enemy force is thrown into disarray, unable to organize itself to clear the boats and make haste, nor to mount any kind of meaningful counterstrike. A few poltroons swim for it, avoiding the enemy's complete annihilation, but nothing of any significance remains intact on the field. Your battle result is a 2.
Blastagarded 12/30/2020 9:35 PM
Excellent work, men!

Narrator 1/25/2021 10:40 PM
The Spring Campaign
10:43 PM
For many years France has worked to keep separate the many branches of the sprawling Habsburg dynasty. The insidious family's control of Spain and the Holy Roman Empire holds France in a vise, and only Cardinal Richelieu's deft maneuvering has kept its jaws from shutting closed. The Habsburgs seek to unite their empire across the Alps, and the corridor for this joining runs through the valley of Valtelline.
10:46 PM
Although the people of Valtellina themselves are Protestant, the Swiss Grison League has aligned itself with France to liberate the region from the hands of the Spanish and Papal armies occupying the valley. With Venice and Savoy also providing support, this is France's best chance to drive a wedge between the Habsburgs and maintain its strategic position in Europe -- a cause worth cooperating with a few Protestants over.
10:48 PM
The Royal Foot Guards will be taking the field, serving alongside the Frontier Regiments, led by the arrogant blowhard Col. Gilles Morançais, himself the abandoned bastard son of a disgraced duke, in flight from gambling arrears.
10:48 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 1/25/2021 10:48 PM
​Narrator rolled 1d6: (1) = 1
1
Narrator 1/25/2021 10:50 PM
This is a Siege campaign. The town of Poschiavo has been fortified and must be taken. Whoever controls Poschiavo controls Valtellina.
10:52 PM
@Blastagarded, Gilles Morançais meets you in the planning tent and offers his opinion of the tactical situation. "Right up the middle," he declares. "Spaniards and Italians. Good for basking in the sun, and not much else. We'll rout them with ease."
2
Blastagarded 1/25/2021 11:20 PM
Then lets use that laziness to our advantage. This is a siege after all. It's their job to stew nervously waiting for us to act. The forces of Venice and Savoy are taking care of their side of the valley, we'll take care of ours. Send messengers advising everyone in the Valley that once the siege begins in earnest, any hint of resistance will be offered no quarter. But for now, anyone who wants to leave the Valley unarmed and in peace may do so. @Curtis Sinnoch Among your other duties, I'm giving you the responsibility over scouting. Ensure we know well in advance of any approaching Spanish reinforcements. Last thing I want to do is get pincered in the very valley we're invading. Fret not, Col. Morançais. We'll be going right up the middle. There's nowhere else to go. Just after we've given them a few days to voluntarily thin their ranks.
11:20 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 1/25/2021 11:20 PM
​Hercule Bonaparte rolled 1d6: (3) = 3
Blastagarded 1/25/2021 11:21 PM
a middling roll for a middling plan
Narrator 1/25/2021 11:23 PM
The Royal Foot Guards take up their positions facing the city walls, taking pot shots here and there, but there seems to be no clear path into the town. Siege equipment and cannon would have been helpful, but each member of the alliance seems to be under the impression that the others were supposed to bring those. The soldiery wastes rounds fighting, and there are a few casualties due to return fire from the walls, but nothing serious. The battle result is a 3 - Inconclusive.
11:24 PM
@Curtis Sinnoch will roll for command of 1st Battalion. @Jules Lavelle will roll for command of 2nd Battalion.
Jules Lavelle 1/25/2021 11:25 PM
The 2nd Battalion has been training for weeks climbing bridges around Paris. No mere town wall can keep us out! Men, charge!
11:25 PM
/roll 1d6

Narrator 1/25/2021 11:26 PM
The 2nd Battalion rushes forward, inexpertly guided by their novice commander. They reach the wall, and some find a handhold, but they are ultimately turned back by a hail of rocks from above. The battle result is a 3 - Inconclusive.

Narrator 1/26/2021 12:23 AM
The bodies of hapless Frontier Regiment soldiers fall to your right and left, but your men stay true. You rush to the gate, suffering few losses, and find it a hasty construction, nothing but the thinnest of wooden planks, with one small iron lock. You smash the lock with the hilt of your cutlass and send the confused Papal soldiers within running for their lives as the gate crashes open. Your result is a 2 - Success with a storming party.

Narrator 1/26/2021 12:26 AM
Where the 2nd Battalion trained to climb, the 1st wisely trained to fight. Your men duck and weave on their approach, expertly dodging enemy fire. As the Royal Marines battalion approaches the gates, you spy a squad of Spaniards lugging a vat of flaming grease to the top of the wall. With one word, your men cease their mad dash and fall to their knees, taking careful aim and firing a volley as one, felling the Spanish devils and setting the top of the wall aflame with spilled grease. Thanks to you, the siege has been a success! Your battle result is 1.

Blastagarded 2/12/2021 9:28 AM
Hercule emerges from seclusion looking not like a man whose be reveling with his mistress for two month, but one whose confidence has been reinvigorate through ample discussion with a like minded companion. Without a word, he hands dispatches to his attaché, before boarding his carriage. The Royal Foot Guard are going to the Front.
Narrator 2/12/2021 9:42 AM
The Minister of War sends a communiqué which reaches the carriage just before leaving Paris. "Col. Bonaparte, It buoys my heart to know of the resolve and zeal with which you would commit your men to the defense of France. However, France is large, and I have already apportioned the appropriate units for the summer campaign. I have determined that your unit is needed to maintain the defense of Paris; as you know, the security of the capital is our chief concern in the face of heavy Habsburg maneuvering. When the danger is less, you are welcome to volunteer your men freely in another season. But for this summer, at least, you are staying put. With resolve, Minister Rouvier"
Blastagarded 2/12/2021 10:17 AM
Hercule turns his carriage around and returns home to spend the month planning a new campaign with his beau: if the Minister of War refuses his brilliance, then the only solution is to become the Minister of War.
4
Jean Renault 2/12/2021 11:20 AM
Meanwhile Mjr. Rochant is leading his troops, helping them avoid planks and cannonballs in the field of battle
Narrator 2/22/2021 11:40 PM
The Summer Campaign
Ghôst Touchard 2/22/2021 11:41 PM
Narrator 2/22/2021 11:45 PM
The Comte de Chalais, Henri de Talleyrand-Périgord, stands accused by Cardinal Richelieu of conspiring to assassinate His Eminence. As the investigation began, the Comte fled to the fortress of the Governor of Brittany and Duke of Vendôme, César de Bourbon, the king's own half-brother.
11:47 PM
Cardinal Richelieu has asked the Minister of War to send a small task force to retrieve these and several other conspirators from Rennes, where they've gone into hiding. Brittany's governor is not without some military might of his own, and as the contingent of Royal troops approaches the city, it becomes clear that they are approaching a fortified target.
11:50 PM
The Field Army is commanded by General Michael Masse, a competent enough leader, known for his reliability, decorum, and above all, luxurious moustaches. (edited)
11:54 PM
The 1st Division is under the leadership of Lt. General Pierre-Paul Auclair, another well-regarded sort, known for a series of clever victories a decade back that he still coasts on to this day.
11:55 PM
The 1st Brigade is commanded by Bg. General Robert Vasseur, who is understood to be the finest foot commander the French Army has to offer.
11:57 PM
General Masse chooses, for his adjutant, the Colonel of the 69th Arquebusiers.
11:58 PM
Lt. General Auclair selects the Lt. Colonel of the Gascon Regiment for his adjutant.
11:59 PM
Maj. Augustin Ladagnous is already serving as the Brigade Major to Bg. General Vasseur.
12:01 AM
@Ghôst Touchard commands the Royal Marines. Sylvestre Côté commands the Picardy Musketeers.
12:03 AM
General Masse arrays his forces. Rennes is small, but walled, and cannon have been allocated at key locations, forming a wall of fire to repel all who might seek to attack the main gate.

The Fall Campaign
1
12:32 PM
Cardinal Richelieu himself has embarked on a secret mission to negotiate a lasting peace with Spain. Fearing English perfidy, he orders the Frontier Regiment to accompany him as an honor guard on his journey to the Principality of Andorra, where he will meet with the Habsburg representative to discuss final terms.
12:35 PM
The Frontier Regiment is commanded by Col. Thierry Boulle, a steady leader whose lack of ambition or imagination is, perhaps, considered an asset by his Red Eminence for the purposes of this undertaking.
12:36 PM
Maj. Augustin Ladagnous has volunteered his Picardy Musketeers 2nd Battalion, including @Abelard Soucy, to serve under Col. Boulle.
1
12:36 PM
The Royal Foot Guards, commanded by @Blastagarded, have also seen fit to tag along, perhaps to keep an eye on the Cardinal for the King.
12:37 PM
The Royal Marines, commanded by @Ghôst Touchard, have attached themselves to the mission, presumably in case any rowboats need boarding.
4

Narrator 4/16/2021 12:52 PM
As French forces cross the border into Andorra, still several miles out from the capital city, they see nothing before them but fields full of toiling farmers. It is a clear day, the sun stands high in the sky, but the farmers keep at their labor.
2

Ghôst Touchard 4/16/2021 12:54 PM

Narrator 4/16/2021 12:54 PM
The Cardinal gives a muffled order from within his coach, and the train pulls to a stop. The assembled soldiery look around in confusion. The fields have gone unnaturally quiet.
12:55 PM
One of the farmers throws down his hoe and grabs an arquebus from where he'd hidden it amidst the cabbage. "FOR SOUBISE!" he cries, and raises his weapon to fire.
12:56 PM
The other farmers similarly draw weapons to attack. The French forces are surrounded, and quickly drop down to defensive positions along the rows of vegetables.
1
12:56 PM
This is a Defence campaign.
12:57 PM
Col. Boulle glances around in confusion, before raising his sword and roaring the order to FIRE!
12:58 PM

12:58 PM
The order is heard clear, and the Frontier Regiment opens up on the attackers, who are cut down in droves, the fire withering them as surely as the sun will wither the untended vegetables.

The Winter Campaign
Narrator
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 7/11/2021 9:19 PM
1d6: (3) = 3
Narrator 7/11/2021 9:19 PM
This will be an Assault campaign.
9:20 PM
Taking the field:
9:21 PM
The Dragoon Brigade, commanded by @Blastagarded, including Grand Duke Max's Dragoons and the Princess Louisa Light Dragoons, itself commanded by @Joseph Bousain.
9:22 PM
The Royal Foot Guards, commanded by @Curtis Sinnoch, with @Jules Lavelle commanding the 1st Battalion.
1
9:23 PM
The Royal Marines, commanded by @Ghôst Touchard, with @Jean Renault leading E Company.
2
9:24 PM
The Frontier Regiment, with @harry serving as a captain, and the Royal Northern Highlands Brigade.
1
9:28 PM
Col. Victor Charlet (MA 4) commands the Frontier Regiment this season.
9:32 PM
It is a time of relative peace. The Spanish and Dutch are occupied with each other. The Savoyards and Genoese have learned their lesson, the Holy Roman Empire is riven with internal conflict, and the strength of the Huguenots has been broken.
9:35 PM
Only one foe threatens the security of France. From across the Channel, the heirs of Guillaume le Conquérant create trouble for God's favored nation.
3
9:36 PM
Under the thinnest of pretexts, the English have seized certain French naval vessels tasked with patrolling coastal areas still rife with Huguenot sympathy.
9:38 PM
The King, furious, gives an ultimatum to the Cardinal. The Cardinal interprets the order, and makes it known to the Minister of War: there will be no official declaration of hostilities.
9:39 PM
Nevertheless, it is understood that some reaction is required, or else France will seem utterly weak. Still, no open conflict with English military forces can be permitted. The Minister settles on a softer target.
9:41 PM
It is a frigid February morning as the French forces make their landing on the beaches of the island of Jersey. The city of Saint-Hélier stands just over a mile's march inland.
9:42 PM
Early-morning fishermen spot the landing, and raise the alarm. The townspeople, confused, move to establish a hasty defense, but know little of the art of fortification. Their only hope lies in the confusion and disarray of the irregular volunteer forces coming against them. (edited)
9:44 PM
@Blastagarded, the regiments under your command depart their boats with impressive speed, given the difficulty of controlling their mounts in the unfamiliar setting.
1
9:45 PM
Give your orders and roll.
Blastagarded 7/11/2021 9:49 PM
We have an impressive force for this assault. While the main armies approach from the front, the dragoons shall divide into two groups and, using their speed, move to compromise and demoralize our foe by position themselves at the enemy's flanks. (edited)
Blastagarded
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 7/11/2021 9:49 PM
1d6: (6) = 6
2
1
Narrator 7/11/2021 9:50 PM
The plan perfectly positions your forces. From far behind the lines, you enjoy the spectacle, and all of the glory. You will be Victorious (BR 1)
9:52 PM
@Joseph Bousain, under Hercule's orders, you sweep your regiment around and find yourself coming upon the surprised townsfolk from the flank. Your initial charge is a crushing success, but the townsfolk understand horse better than musket, and the fighting soon becomes fierce. Roll for your battle result.
1
9:53 PM
While we wait for Robert...
9:54 PM
@Curtis Sinnoch, your men disembark, orderly and well-equipped as always, but there is a long march through unfamiliar terrain ahead, and an alerted force-at-arms at the other end. Give your orders and roll.
2
Curtis Sinnoch 7/11/2021 10:00 PM
"Gentlemen! These English Pig-dogs dare sully the honor of glorious France! We have the Cardinal's (and Gods!) blessing - the city of Saint-Hélier will fall before the might of the Royal Food Guards! @Jules Lavelle - you take your Battalion in a forwarding scouting sortie - crush any brutes you may find, but fall back should they be well entrenched. Second battalion, follow 15 minutes behind Lavelle, sending a forward party to confirm with the rangers before committing the rest. 3rd Battalion, flank to either side and form a minor rearguard to ensure we do not get intercepted. TO GLORY!
Narrator 7/11/2021 10:05 PM
@Curtis Sinnoch, your roll, please.
Curtis Sinnoch
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 7/11/2021 10:05 PM
1d6: (1) = 1
2
Narrator 7/11/2021 10:07 PM
The 1st Battalion takes its scouting mission seriously, scattering and hounding out every last noise from the strange underbrush. As a result, the 2nd has nobody to follow, and wanders uselessly towards the town, never quite reaching it. The 3rd splits into groups and stares at the boats on the shore. The boatmen refuse to share their breakfast, and a small fight breaks out. (edited)
3

10:11 PM
The fighting is horrific, but when it concludes, the vile English stain has been utterly wiped from the forest.

10:11 PM

@Ghôst Touchard, the impressed fishing boats that carried you to the island land with the rest at the eastern shore. But your Marines may be prepared for an amphibious assault on the city's docks directly. The choice is up to you.
Ghôst Touchard 7/11/2021 10:14 PM
The Royal Marines shall remain a unified fighting force in order to bolster our chances at victory. That being said, while our goal is always the glory of France, we seek to line our pockets with as much English gold as we can acquire. We prepare for an amphibious assault on the city's docks directly!

Narrator 7/11/2021 10:21 PM
The docks are silent and empty as your men row up to make their landing. Some dozen upturned barrels are the only sign of activity, and the various equipment of fishing boats that should be stored here cannot be found. A strange smell rises from the water. One of your men dips his hand in. "Fish oil?" he asks, puzzled.
2
Jean Renault 7/11/2021 10:21 PM
Uh oh
Ghôst Touchard 7/11/2021 10:22 PM
Sorry, new recruit..
Narrator 7/11/2021 10:22 PM
A toothless old man pops out from a barrel, cackling, and throws open the lantern he carries, plunging it into the water. The oily surface ignites, and so too do the docks, your boats, and your men.
2
10:23 PM
The man continues to laugh as he himself catches fire. The marines shout and leap from the boats. A few lucky ones make the dock, and are able to rush ashore before the flames can catch them. Those who land in the water are less fortunate, burning alive amidst the lapping waves. The oil soaks into their uniforms, ensuring that even if they submerge themselves, they reignite as soon as they surface once more.
1
10:24 PM
The few stragglers who arrive in the town proper find it deserted, theirs for the taking. Your result is Pyrrhic victory (BR 3)

@Joseph Bousain, under Hercule's orders, you sweep your regiment around and find yourself coming upon the surprised townsfolk from the flank. Narrator 7/12/2021 10:29 AM
@Joseph Bousain, much to your shock, the townspeople are far better organized than expected. They fall back after brief fighting, and your men charge, only to discover the retreat was a feint. The civilians throw up a wall of hastily-assembled wooden spears, more than enough to ward off your cavalry, and harass your men with thrown sticks and stones, sending them back towards their initial position. The standoff only ends when the town erupts into angry orange flames behind its defenders. Your result is Inconclusive (BR 4)

Narrator 7/30/2021 3:02 PM
The Spring Campaign
Narrator 7/30/2021 3:10 PM
Deploying: -@Blastagarded's Dragoon Brigade, including @Joseph Bousain in Princess Louisa's Light Dragoons -@Ghôst Touchard's 1st Brigade of Foot, including @Abelard Soucy in the Picardy Musketeers -@Curtis Sinnoch's Royal Foot Guards, including @Jules Lavelle -@Jean Renault's Crown Prince Cuirassiers, 3rd Squadron -@laiv.wire's 53rd Fusiliers, 3rd Battalion (acting) And assorted other squadrons/battalions.
3:12 PM
The English are at it once more. The filthy sons of Albion spread their vile, corrupting touch across the very heartland of France. The English have sent the provacateur Walter Montagu to stir dissent among the nobility against the French monarchy. He has raised funds and commitments of soldiers to support a new force to be commanded by France's old foe, the Duke of Soubise himself.
Narrator
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 7/30/2021 3:13 PM
1d6: (3) = 3
Narrator 7/30/2021 3:15 PM
Loyalists among the manor staff of Claude, Duke of Chevreuse (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Lorrain.jpg), have reported to the Cardinal's agents of a concentration of forces and supplies on his very lands a mere two days' march from Paris. (edited)
3:16 PM
Chevreuse's castle, the Château de la Madeleine, is a fortified hilltop redoubt with a military tradition over 500 years old. Despite its age, it has been consistently maintained and modernized, and the walls are reinforced to withstand cannonades.
3:17 PM
Although the castle is well-protected and will require a siege, the men and matériel intended for the war effort are scattered throughout the town and countryside of Chevreuse, and therefore vulnerable to Assault.
3:21 PM
The Royal forces arrive from the north-east. There will be fighting in both town and field, to be sure. The enemy was alerted shortly after the army left Paris, and has lined the hills and fields with hastily-assembled earthworks and their accompanying trenches. The town of Chevreuse, too, has been fortified, with blockades and traps lining the streets, ready to harass and slow any advancing force.
3:22 PM
The top commanding officers on the field, @Blastagarded, @Ghôst Touchard, @Curtis Sinnoch, @Jean Renault, and @laiv.wire must array their forces, give their orders, and roll.
3:23 PM
1
Ghôst Touchard 7/30/2021 3:27 PM

Narrator 7/30/2021 3:49 PM
Pépin wisely stands far back from the obvious trap. As fragments of teeth and bones of the two scouts rain down on the assembled regiment, he shields his eyes from the downpour of debris. Had he kept them uncovered, he may have seen the smoldering, twirling two-by-four, somehow intact after the explosion, falling down towards where he stands. It whizzes past, but on the backturn makes solid contact with his skull, and Pépin's battle, and life, are over.
6
1
Blastagarded 7/30/2021 3:50 PM
Taking inspiration from their opponent's in the previous campaign, Hercule inspects the Dragoons personally before they depart France. In particular, the specialized incendiaries each soldier has been issued. There is only one thing to do with English who invade our lands and those who tolerate their presence: burn the cancer out. Hercule instructs his forces that for this assault, the Dragoons will be adopting the role of the Chevauchée. Using their speed, they will swing wide from the main front to approach from the west, where they will push until they encounter halting resistance. At that point, from their halted position, they will set fire to everything and let the purifying flames conduct the rest of their grim work. With luck, the conflagration may even threaten the castle. (edited)

Narrator 7/30/2021 3:51 PM
The Royal Foot Guards advance slowly and surely towards the town. The fighting is fierce, the town well defended. but Chevreuse always expected its castle to weather attacks, and the steadfast training of the Guards overwhelms the poorly-drilled traitor detritus Soubise has so far assembled. The fighting is hard, but the result undeniable. Curtis enjoys a Bloody victory (BR 2).
3:54 PM
It is hard riding, but the Dragoon Brigade's main force keeps clear of overhanging branches, and most of them survive the maneuver to enact their commander's plan. As the bulk of the resistance is positioned to the east, none but the peasantry remain to raise the alarm, ignored, as their homes and livelihoods go up in flames. Hercule enjoys a Bloody victory (BR 2).

Narrator 7/30/2021 4:22 PM
As Hercule watches the fires burn, the glint in his eyes seems otherworldly. The burning horses running back towards him do nothing to stir the man with ice in his veins. Alongside the fleeing Light Dragoons, one strange rider goes unnoticed, tearing out of the flames. Hercule spots something strange about the man, and raises his sword. The hooded Duke of Soubise draws a pistol and fires a ball straight into the Brigadier-General's chest. Hercule falls from his horse without a sound, and the Duke flees into the night. The creeping flames overtake the noble Knight, and nothing remains but a dream of ambition.

Narrator 7/30/2021 6:03 PM
After the battle, Henri picks over the bodies of the fallen for loot. Under a pile of dead townsfolk, a small sound is heard. Curious, he digs down, and finds a young woman, sobbing, having been trapped beneath her dead family for nearly an hour. Henri takes the girl into his tent to see to any wounds she has. She seems unharmed, but drinks a glass of wine and falls dead asleep. Henri focuses on paperwork, after-action reports and death notices. He remains so engrossed that he doesn't notice the girl awaken a couple hours later. She slips out of the bedroll, reaches for a shaving razor, and gives Henri the last shave he will ever receive.
2
1
laiv.wire 7/30/2021 6:04 PM
Finally, the warm touch of a belle chere...
Narrator 7/30/2021 6:18 PM
The Spring Campaign has concluded. Another town burned, another victory for France.
1
6:20 PM
The castle surrenders immediately after the town is taken, without need for a siege. The Duke of Chevreuse successfully pins the blame on his wife, Marie de Rohan, claiming she went behind his back to work with Walter Montagu and Soubise. To the great fury of the Cardinal, Soubise is nowhere to be found in castle or town.
1
Narrator 8/21/2021 9:10 PM
The Summer Campaign
9:16 PM
The summer heat will stir even the most languid foe into open conflict with the right-hearted favorites of God. The army is once again deployed to the fortress-island of Ré. The English, dissatisfied with the progress in the Huguenot rebellion they've so vigorously encouraged, and seeking to lift the siege on La Rochelle by any means necessary, have landed several thousand men on the Île de Ré. With the Duke of Buckingham himself commanding the English forces, they have been able to swiftly cover the grounds of the island generally, with only the star fort keeping them at bay. Buckingham has settled in for a siege, and only brave smugglers, incentivized by wise bounties from Cardinal Richelieu, have been able to keep the fortress supplied.
9:17 PM
The army arrives as part of a rescue mission, seeking to break the siege and save the courageous defenders of Saint-Martin-de-Ré. The full might of the Royal Army crashing onto the shores ought to shatter the morale of any enemy, but Buckingham orders what defenders he can to go to ground, spreading themselves over the island to fight man-to-man where necessary. (edited)
9:19 PM
Royal forces are organized as one enormous army, commanded by Gen. Serge Vaganay, regarded as the most dynamic and brilliant military mind ever to lead a French army in battle.
9:19 PM
Beneath Gen. Vaganay, forces are arrayed as follows: (edited)
9:19 PM
9:19 PM
9:20 PM
9:20 PM
9:20 PM
As well as the Frontier Division and Regiment.
9:24 PM
The general's adjutants produce a hastily-drawn map of the island and the known disposition of English forces.
9:24 PM
Narrator 8/21/2021 11:25 PM
All units are performing Field Operations.
11:26 PM
Gen. Vaganay has tussled with Buckingham before. He surveys the map, and compares it to the marshy ground stretching before him. It's mid-morning before the Royal forces are finished disembarking and sufficiently organized to begin the attack. The explosions of cannon echo over the waves from the sea battle taking place just off the island.
11:29 PM
If Saint-Martin is to be saved, it will require all his arts. He summons his division commanders. The 1st Division will stay in reserve, to reinforce where needed. The 2nd Division will make up the main attack force, spreading out to search and destroy the enemy. The Cavalry Division will range ahead, spotting and engaging only where necessary. The Dragoon Brigade will be sent in as a surgical strike force, seeking to circle around and cut off the English forces in the field from their command camp by the siege zone.
Narrator
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 8/21/2021 11:29 PM
1d6: (2) = 2
Narrator 8/21/2021 11:32 PM
The orders are textbook. The battlefield is not. The cavalry finds itself terribly slowed by the marshy ground. Without their intelligence, the infantry are set upon from all sides as they advance, though at least they come expecting it. The result is Inconclusive (BR 4).
11:35 PM
Lt. Gen. Bernard Lefebvre, a distant cousin of @Blastagarded, has earned great respect for his sober, direct leadership and absolute refusal to surrender. He deploys his infantry to cover the rear with great precision and care, setting up a defensive perimeter around the landing zone designed to be completely impenetrable to enemy spies and saboteurs. (edited)
Narrator
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 8/21/2021 11:35 PM
1d6: (5) = 5
1
Narrator 8/21/2021 11:37 PM
Just in time, as the final hastily-assembled fencing goes up, a squadron of English dragoons attempt to slip through the picket. Unseen ropes tangle their horses' legs, and they go crashing to the ground, where they are easily set upon and slaughtered. Their comrades, watching from a distance, turn and run, leaving the landing site unmolested. The result is Enemy driven from field (BR 2).
11:40 PM
As he watches the enemy dragoons flee, Bg. Gen. Frank Bouchard, commanding the Guards Brigade, feels a tap on his shoulder. His adjutant has noticed some civilians wandering towards their lines, and is asking what they should do.
Narrator
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 8/21/2021 11:41 PM
1d6: (3) = 3
Narrator 8/21/2021 11:44 PM
Bg. Gen. Bouchard orders his men to ignore the civilians. His adjutant, feeling suspicious, approaches to inspect them anyway. The seemingly ragtag group throws aside their cloaks to reveal they've come packing pistols. They begin firing into the astonished Guards, but the adjutant raises his own rapier skewers several, taking a musket ball for his trouble. The Guards are left mostly intact thanks to the adjutant's quick thinking. The result is Inconclusive (BR 4).
11:47 PM
Col. @Curtis Sinnoch's Royal Foot Guards stand watch as the lower-ranking regiments clean up the mess from the civilian attack. He notices the sounds of the sea battle getting closer. Then, without further warning, an English galleon appears from behind a stony cliff, its decks aflame, bearing down directly on the landing site. Crazed English sailors, resigned to their fate, fire muskets from the rigging down into the assembled Guards. Col. Sinnoch, declare your orders and roll!
11:52 PM
As the Guards Brigade deals with emergencies from behind the lines, the 1st Brigade of Foot under Bg. Gen. @Ghôst Touchard prepares to reinforce units in the field. Rather than wait for calls, the regimental commanders request to be set loose to maneuver, actively seeking out opportunities to reinforce, rather than risk being too late. Bg. Gen. Thibodeaux, declare your orders and roll!
Ghôst Touchard 8/21/2021 11:57 PM
The 1st Brigade of Foot will hold position awaiting calls for reinforcement in order to be deployed in a strategic manner. We will not split into guerrilla forces so a few commanders can gain glory at the expense of my men's lives.
Ghôst Touchard
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 8/21/2021 11:57 PM
1d6: (6) = 6
Narrator 8/21/2021 11:58 PM
Your orders prove prescient; not a single call for reinforcements comes, except from the Guards Brigade stationed behind you. The landing site would've been completely overrun, the invasion force stranded, but for your wise forbearance. Your result is Enemy crushed (BR 1).
2
12:04 AM
Cavalry Division commander Lt. Gen. Jérémie De Michele, a young man of illustrious lineage, orders his brigadier generals to approach the scouting mission as though they were scouting attackers on Paris itself. Though the wet ground presents issues for the horses, thoroughness is considered more important than speed.
Narrator
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 8/22/2021 12:04 AM
1d6: (5) = 5
Narrator 8/22/2021 12:05 AM
Gen. Vaganay's subordinates are proving themselves much more adept than their commander. The enemy are quickly spotted and targeted for destruction, with the cavalry avoiding direct conflict and following the plan of battle impeccably. The result is Enemy driven from field (BR 2).
12:07 AM
Horse Guards commander Bg. Gen. Denis Nee must choose between keeping his regiments near the shore, or pressing inland. He selects the shore, leaving the inland for the Heavy Brigade.
Narrator
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 8/22/2021 12:07 AM
1d6: (3) = 3
Narrator 8/22/2021 12:08 AM
The enemy have avoided the shore almost entirely, pressing inland to lay their traps, either to avoid bombardment or make it harder to spot them. No matter the why, little glory accrues to the Horse Guards. The result is Inconclusive (BR 3).
12:11 AM
The Heavy Brigade, under Bg. Gen. Jean-Martin Desjardins, performs the needed scouting inland. The island is criss-crossed with small streams, rocky outcroppings, and woods, making it truly treacherous terrain for cavalry. Desjardins has been ordered against speed, but his men and horses are agitated at the unwelcoming site of battle.
Narrator
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 8/22/2021 12:11 AM
1d6: (5) = 5
Narrator 8/22/2021 12:12 AM
The increased pace proves essential for the outcome of the battle. The Heavy Brigade encounters an entire enemy division marching double-time to reach pre-built defenses, and delivers their location with speed enough to avoid the 2nd Brigade marching straight into the death trap. The result is Enemy driven from field (BR 2).
12:14 AM
Col. Gaël Roquebert of the Crown Prince Cuirassiers, a man of duty and honor, follows the order to disengage once the enemy division has been spotted. Even so, during the retreat, an advance force of English cuirassiers comes sweeping into their flanks. Col. Roquebert frantically orders his troops to wheel around and battle for their lives.
Narrator
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 8/22/2021 12:14 AM
1d6: (5) = 5
Narrator 8/22/2021 12:16 AM
The men and horse prove themselves disciplined, and the good order that characterizes the attack permeates the performance of the regiment. They make quick work of the starved, howling English. The result is Enemy driven from field (BR 2).
12:18 AM
Maj. @Jean Renault of the Crown Prince Cuirassiers finds his squadron separated from the rest of the force by a single Troop of enemy cavalry. The crazed captain shouts something in his ugly Germanic tongue. He seems to be challenging Jean to, could it be, a joust? Jean, give your orders and roll. (edited)
1
12:21 AM
The Dragoon Brigade, temporarily commanded by Col. Martin Breguet of Princess Louisa's Light Dragoons, attempts to slip past enemy patrols to find its way between the siege and the enemy force proper. Breguet, although inexperienced as a general officer, nevertheless possesses one of the keenest military minds the Dragoons have ever produced.
Narrator
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 8/22/2021 12:21 AM
1d6: (3) = 3
Narrator 8/22/2021 12:22 AM
The plan fails; the Dragoons are spotted before they can even get close to the siege zone. But Breguet's expertise in regimental command enables him to organize a fighting withdrawal that averts disaster for his men. The result is Inconclusive (BR 3).
12:25 AM
With Col. Breguet running the brigade, it falls to Brevet Lt. Colonel @Joseph Bousain to lead Princess Louisa's Light Dragoons in battle. The alarm has been called, and hordes of enemy soldiers are pouring towards your embattled line. As Col. Breguet screams for you to disengage, your captains insist that these filthy English can be beaten, if only you can find the courage to stand and fight. Robert, give your orders and roll!
Joseph Bousain 8/22/2021 4:56 AM
"Men! These roast beef protestants are nothing to be afraid of! Let us show them the fiercesomeness of fighting Frenchman!"
4:58 AM
Robert calculates that the English are just far enough away for his dragoons to dismount and fire two volleys before remounting and charging directly into the centre of their line.

1d6: (4) = 4
Curtis Sinnoch 8/22/2021 7:20 AM
"Solider of the Royal Foot Guard! See how this ship is full of dead men - they just don't know it yet! They will come with reckless yet fierce abandon - cover your brothers in arms, keep your head down, and allow this foe to be delivered to God himself! FIRE!"
1
Curtis Sinnoch
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 8/22/2021 7:20 AM
1d6: (4) = 4
Narrator 8/22/2021 1:35 PM
@Joseph Bousain's orders are carried out perfectly, and his men even manage a full three volleys, sending the mad English reeling. Their charge, however, is met with surprising readiness by another fresh line behind the initial wave, who repel the dragoons in short order. The withdrawal continues as planned. The result is Inconclusive (BR 3).
2
1:40 PM
@Curtis Sinnoch's Royal Foot Guard arrange themselves behind whatever cover the landing site offers. They return fire at the doomed English sailors, but before long, even the cannons of the flaming galleon begin to roar, scattering men and materiél across the shoreline. All seems lost until the disciplined volleys of the RFG land a lucky shot on the ship's pilot, who slumps against the wheel. The enemy vessel turns sharply, and instead of its inertia carrying it onto the beach, it falls onto its side in the shallows. English sailors are sent careening into the tide, the flames of their fury falling fruitlessly into the sea, to be shot dead by the Guard before they can rise again. The result is Enemy driven from field (BR 2).
2
1:41 PM
Maj. @Jules Lavelle takes the initiative, ordering his 1st Battalion to wade into the water to finish off any survivors.

The Fall Campaign
Narrator
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 9/28/2021 1:56 PM
1d6: (6) = 6
Narrator 9/28/2021 1:57 PM
This will be a Field Operations campaign.
2
Narrator 9/28/2021 2:09 PM
After suffering miserable defeat under the leadership of Duke Soubise, the city of La Rochelle was thought to have learned its lesson, and become an ardent bastion of the Royalists. However, under pressure from the vile Buckingham and his fleet's blockade, and the besieging Huguenot army, the city's masters have broken down, opening the gates to let in Buckingham and his cohort. Cardinal Richelieu himself has come to direct the siege efforts. With the bulk of the regular army devoted to the siege, the Cardinal has directed the Minister of War to allocate certain regiments to support the Frontier Regiment in scouring the countryside for lingering Huguenot forces bent on breaking the siege.
Narrator 9/28/2021 2:17 PM
Deploying to the field are: The 1st Brigade of Foot, under Bg. General @Ghôst Touchard; The Royal Foot Guards, under Marquis-Colonel @Curtis Sinnoch, with Lt. Col. @Jules Lavelle commanding the 1st Battalion; Princess Louisa's Light Dragoons, under Lt. Col. @Joseph Bousain, with Cap. @Abelard Soucy leading F Troop; Pvt. @CitrusNotes serving in the Gascon Regiment's E Company; and Pvt. @Blastagarded attached to a very confused Frontier Regiment. (edited)
2:18 PM
The Frontier Regiment is commanded by Col. Melvin Duret, a nobleman of slightly too-certain breeding. (MA 2) (edited)
2:20 PM
Although the Huguenots are scattered across the landscape, they are currently well-supplied and moving in cohesive military units. With their leadership holed up in La Rochelle, the rebels' hopes lie entirely on the English-supported field units meeting the Royal Army in open battle and emerging victorious to lift the siege.
2:22 PM
Royalist forces will be attempting to seek-and-destroy the enemy over eighty square miles of wide-open Aquitaine farmland.
2:23 PM
As the ranking officer in the field, @Ghôst Touchard will detail the plan of deployment and roll.
Ghôst Touchard 9/28/2021 2:28 PM
The Royal Marines will be aiding in the siege effort to prevent the rejoining of the rebels and their leaders while the Picardy Musketeers will be systematically hunting the rebels in the nearby farmland.
Ghôst Touchard
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 9/28/2021 2:29 PM
1d6: (6) = 6
Narrator 9/28/2021 2:36 PM
The 1st Brigade immediately makes contact with multiple enemy regiments. The Picardy Musketeers engage in pitched battle, and even manage to draw attention from the Royal Marines, who succeed in withdrawing to assist the siege. The Marines arrive just in time to rebuff a desperate sally by the defenders, successfully capturing George Villiers, the Duke of Buckingham himself, in the midst of an escape attempt.
2:37 PM
The result is 1 - Enemy crushed.
3
2:37 PM
@Curtis Sinnoch, please deploy your forces and roll.
Narrator 9/28/2021 2:46 PM
@Joseph Bousain, please deploy your forces and roll.
2:51 PM
Maj. Servais Poussin, stolid and unpretentious commander of the Gascon Regiment's 3rd Battalion, leads his men with grim determination. Spotting a line of enemy forces across a field of asparagus, he orders a charge.
Narrator
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 9/28/2021 2:51 PM
1d6: (1) = 1
1
Narrator 9/28/2021 2:53 PM
The enemy line is revealed to be a line of tactically-placed scarecrows. The 3rd Battalion opts to vent their frustrations on them anyway, screaming and bayoneting the silent guardians. They little notice the approach of a Huguenot force, who open fire from a mere 30 yards away, tearing huge holes in the line and sending the battalion scattering. The result is 6 - Own crushed.

Blastagarded 9/28/2021 3:10 PM
Unlike his compatriots, Sebastien knows how to hunt in a farmers field. Keeping low to the ground and ears perked, he slowly moves towards the sound he's recognized since childhood, that of cooing grouse. Avoiding disturbing the flock, he raises his rifle and thinks to himself, "Tonight, I will eat well."
Blastagarded
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 9/28/2021 3:11 PM
2d6: (6+4) = 10
2
1
Narrator 9/28/2021 3:17 PM
The grouse is finished. But its flock has long grown accustomed to the sound of gunfire, and what it means for their prospects for a meal in the midst of a battlefield. They sprint towards the sound of the shot, in great numbers. As Sebastien smugly observes the fallen bird, another of its kind pops its head out of the underbrush, softly cooing the last sound he will ever hear. His final thought, before being torn to shreds, is"petite futée…" (edited)

Robert decides to lead the charge into the withdrawing enemy. He pays little attention to the greenery of the field, where the Huguenots have scattered caltrops. His horse shatters a hoof on one, sending him soaring through the air. Miraculously, he lands unharmed some thirty feet ahead. Less miraculously, he finds himself surrounded by the enemy. They attempt to capture him, but the brave Lieutenant-Colonel refuses to be taken alive.

Narrator 10/4/2021 12:18 AM
The battle is a great success, forcing the final field elements of the English-supported Huguenots back into the city. La Rochelle remains under siege, but with Cardinal Richelieu directing the construction of great siege engines, a firm naval blockade, and plans for further fortifications already in motion, the city's fall is only a matter of time.
Narrator
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 11/5/2021 12:25 AM
1d6: (5) = 5
Narrator 11/5/2021 12:26 AM
The Winter Campaign
12:32 AM
With the Huguenots contained in La Rochelle, the bulk of the army is pulled back to Paris to defend the capital. This is fortuitous, as even with the English navy held off at sea, and Buckingham in chains, a new English threat has presented itself. A swarm of new baronets, created by Charles V to bolster the officer corps of the military, have taken it upon themselves to seize King Louis himself. Landing in Normandy, the new lords and their household forces have made quick work of the meager northern coastal defenses and set inland. Thankfully, they lack common leadership, and have split up across the bocage to pillage and raid on their way to the capital. With the Cardinal overseeing the siege at La Rochelle, the King himself has condescended to order the army to defend his royal person at all costs. This will be a Field Operation.
12:36 AM
Forces Deploying: -Bg. Gen. @Ghôst Touchard in command of the 1st Brigade of Foot -Marquis Col. @Curtis Sinnoch in command of the Royal Foot Guards, including Lt. Col. @Jules Lavelle -Maj. @Jean Renault in command of the Crown Prince Cuirassiers 3rd Squadron -Cap. @Abelard Soucy leading the Princess Louisa's Light Dragoons C Troop -Sub. @CitrusNotes with the Frontier Regiment -@Joseph Bousain, without rank, who has been temporarily assigned the rank of Private in the Frontier Regiment
12:38 AM
In command of the Frontier Regiment is Col. Gabriel Kaplan, once an able commander, now a blithering fool after taking a musket ball to the cranium.
12:39 AM
As the senior officer on the field, it falls to @Ghôst Touchard to provide the overall plan of battle.
12:40 AM
The enemy is loosely arranged in pockets scattered over the countryside. No frontage presents itself; the tall hedges separating one field from the next guarantee isolated fighting. The rising smoke and screaming peasants make it a trivial matter to find the enemy.
12:41 AM
@Jean Renault, @Curtis Sinnoch are invited to give their orders and roll as well. (edited)
12:42 AM
Col. Bertrand Beauvais of Princess Louisa's Light Dragoons recognizes the proper use of cavalry in a wide-ranging battle like this, and assigns his forces on a series of small skirmish and containment missions, refusing to directly engage the enemy.
Narrator
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 11/5/2021 12:42 AM
1d6: (3) = 3
Narrator 11/5/2021 12:43 AM
The plan works perfectly, and the enemy, once routed, finds they have nowhere to go. The result is 2 - Enemy driven from field.
12:44 AM
@Abelard Soucy, your rolls are Death 11, Mention 10, Promotion 9, Crowns 8.
12:49 AM
Col. Gabriel Kaplan rides up and down the line, gesticulating wildly in the direction of some hedges. Whatever he means to say is lost in a cascade of spittle that soaks his uniform. He attempts to make up for a lack of articulation with pure fighting spirit.
Narrator
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 11/5/2021 12:49 AM
1d6: (3) = 3
Narrator 11/5/2021 12:51 AM
He fails. He raises his sword, giving the order for the Frontier Regiment to charge the hedgerow. Of course, all his wild shouts have given plenty of warning to the English forces, who open fire from behind cover.
12:51 AM
The result is 5 - Own driven from field.

Jerome Greybeck, eager to prove himself, leads the charge. His big moment has finally arrived. The biggest gamble of his life is about to pay off.
12:58 AM
With his comrades dropping like flies around him, he manages to enter the hedgerow itself, only to be tangled in its vines. The treacherous Norman countryside has him in its grips. He makes easy pickings for the English bayonets.
2
Abelard Soucy 11/5/2021 1:01 AM
Primo, has taken to hard living for the past few months and practiced an ascetic regime after experiencing the loss of his lady love as well as his social standing due to his cowardice. Cowardice, which, he reasoned, came from soft city living. Now, hardened, and with his soul right with God, he takes Reckless Bravery for 2. He leads the Dragoons on, and is the first to dismount and engage an isolated pocket of enemies.
Abelard Soucy
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 11/5/2021 1:02 AM
2d6 death: (6+3) = 9
Narrator 11/5/2021 1:04 AM
Primo feels confident under such a seasoned commander. He holds his place aside his horse without the fear that has haunted him before. He is where he is meant to be. Even as the sharp crack of musket fire heralds the approaching enemy, he steadies his pistol and readies for the fight.
CitrusNotes 11/5/2021 1:04 AM
Bleeding profusely and losing all sense, Jerome sees a small vine of ripe berries growing at his feet. One last meal, he thinks as he weakly scoops a few into his mouth. A final throw off the dice. They were, of course, poisonous, but you must have blood in your body for that to matter...
2
Narrator 11/5/2021 1:06 AM
Primo's horse is less ready. As the volley of incoming fire spatters harmlessly into the ground before the waiting dragoons, his horse spooks, and turns to run. In the process, it catches a stirrup against Primo's sword, pulling him to the ground and dragging him behind into a field of waiting thistles.
1:07 AM
What returns to camp, still dragged behind the horse, is not recognizable as a soldier or a man, but the grooms know the horse well.

Narrator 11/5/2021 10:07 PM
